metadata.title: The phasor that spins but never changes anything
metadata.purpose: vox-explainer slate cut of Quantum Mechanics Vol. 5 candidate 01. Source: M-01 (Complex Numbers and the Complex Exponential), stationary-state phasor section.
metadata.clock: narration (ElevenLabs) — durations below are word-count estimates until GATE 0 audio lock
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL=rotating phasor (unit-circle, no change in probability); CRIMSON=naive expectation that stationary means nothing moves
metadata.source: Vol. 5, M-01 (Complex Numbers and the Complex Exponential), In the Quantum Series — stationary-state phasor
metadata.note: Exclusions: do not drift into full time-dependent Schrodinger evolution; keep to one eigenstate; do not cover superposition interference; do not discuss global vs relative phase distinction

beats.narration_text: Physics calls it a stationary state. Stationary means nothing changes. But write the wavefunction down and it never stops spinning.

card.dek: The phasor that spins but never changes anything

beats.narration_text: An energy eigenstate rotates in the complex plane at rate E over h-bar. Its probability stays flat. How can something rotate and change nothing?

card.dek: e^{-iEt/hbar} rotates without stop. |psi|^2 never moves. Why?

beats.narration_text: The wavefunction psi lives in the complex plane — a two-component thing with a real part and an imaginary part. When time passes, the state rotates.
The time factor is e to the minus-i-E-t-over-hbar. That is a unit arrow. Its length is exactly one. Always.
Probability is not psi — it is psi-star times psi, the modulus squared. When you multiply a unit arrow by its conjugate, the phases cancel exactly.
Watch: psi rotates left, psi-star rotates right by the same amount. Their product stays pinned at the same real number, no matter the angle.
The spatial part psi-of-x carries all the shape information. The time factor is a pure phase — it dresses the shape but contributes zero to any probability.
Stationary does not mean frozen in the complex plane. It means every observable — every number you can measure — stays constant in time.

props.headline: Stationary does not mean frozen in the complex plane. It means…

beats.narration_text: Illustrative: hydrogen ground state, energy minus 13.6 eV. The phasor sweeps at 2 times ten to the 16 radians per second. Probability density: one Bohr radius, locked still.
The phasor spins at unit length. Probability is the square of the modulus. A unit circle squared is one. Stationary means the observable is frozen, not the state.

card.dek: Unit rotation — probability unchanged. Stationary = observable frozen.

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: Part of the Quantum Mechanics: Math for Quantum Physics series.
